reasoning: While the composite fidelity score is high, the conversion has omitted critical context including the document's title and metadata footer. The corruption of the legend into a single table creates false semantic relationships between unrelated columns, which constitutes a structural defect that goes beyond cosmetic styling and requires human intervention to ensure the map remains interpretable and accessible.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The output fails to capture a significant amount of textual information present in the source image. Missing items include the top banner text ('NC DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC SAFETY', 'Emergency Management'), the map's internal title and date ('Risk Building Footprint Refresh', 'Current Status 6/16/2025'), and the metadata footer ('Scale: 1:3,179,372', '1 inch equals 50 miles', 'Spatial Reference Name: NAD 1983 StatePlane North Carolina FIPS 3200 Feet', 'Status as of: 6/16/2025'). Collectively, these
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source contains the following text: 'Risk Building Footprint Refresh', 'Current Status 6/16/2025', 'Scale: 1:3,179,372', '1 inch equals 50 miles', 'Spatial Reference Name: NAD 1983 StatePlane North Carolina FIPS 3200 Feet', 'Status as of: 6/16/2025', 'NC DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC SAFETY', 'Emergency Management'.
concerns.output_quote: The text identified in the source is entirely absent from the JSON elements.

concerns.analysis: The legend section of the source document consists of two independent columns of information: one for Project Phases (left) and one for Status Types (right). The conversion process forced this visual layout into a single data table, creating arbitrary row-wise pairings (e.g., 'Phase 1 (2020)' paired with 'In Progress'). In the source, these items are not semantically linked in a row-based relationship; they are separate legend categories. This structural corruption misrepresents the logic of the
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The legend in the PDF has two separate columns: Left column lists Phases 1-4 and 'Not Started'; Right column lists status states like 'In Progress' and 'Initial QC Complete'.
concerns.output_quote: The JSON table (element index 1) pairs these into rows: ["Phase 1 (2020)", "In Progress"], ["Phase 2 (2021)", "Initial QC Complete"], etc.

action_items: Restore the missing map title and technical metadata (scale, spatial reference, and date) to the document.
Deconstruct the legend table and reformat as two separate lists or properly labeled columns to reflect the original semantic structure.
